subject: Mathematics
title: Fibonacci and the Golden Ratio
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Fibonacci and the Golden Ratio.

hook.text: Divide consecutive Fibonacci numbers: 8/5 = 1.6, 13/8 = 1.625, 21/13 = 1.615. A pattern emerges.

buildup.text: These ratios converge to a specific number: 1.6180339... the golden ratio, called phi.

discovery.text: The golden ratio satisfies a unique equation: phi equals 1 plus 1 over phi.

twist.text: You can express any Fibonacci number exactly using a formula involving phi—even though phi is irrational.

climax.text: This formula, called Binet's formula, links the simple Fibonacci rule to deep algebraic structure.

punchline.text: Add whole numbers and an irrational constant emerges.

quiz.question: What value do consecutive Fibonacci ratios approach?
